title: CDMGNotation
lean_code_with_comments:
instance instMembership : Membership Node (CDMG Node) where
  mem G v := v ∈ G.J ∪ G.V

def tuh (G : CDMG Node) (v1 v2 : Node) : Prop := (v1, v2) ∈ G.E

def hut (G : CDMG Node) (v1 v2 : Node) : Prop := (v2, v1) ∈ G.E

def huh (G : CDMG Node) (v1 v2 : Node) : Prop := (v1, v2) ∈ G.L

def suh (G : CDMG Node) (v1 v2 : Node) : Prop :=
  G.tuh v1 v2 ∨ G.huh v1 v2

def hus (G : CDMG Node) (v1 v2 : Node) : Prop :=
  G.hut v1 v2 ∨ G.huh v1 v2

def sus (G : CDMG Node) (v1 v2 : Node) : Prop :=
  G.tuh v1 v2 ∨ G.hut v1 v2 ∨ G.huh v1 v2
lean_code_without_comments:
instance instMembership : Membership Node (CDMG Node) where
  mem G v := v ∈ G.J ∪ G.V

def tuh (G : CDMG Node) (v1 v2 : Node) : Prop := (v1, v2) ∈ G.E

def hut (G : CDMG Node) (v1 v2 : Node) : Prop := (v2, v1) ∈ G.E

def huh (G : CDMG Node) (v1 v2 : Node) : Prop := (v1, v2) ∈ G.L

def suh (G : CDMG Node) (v1 v2 : Node) : Prop :=
  G.tuh v1 v2 ∨ G.huh v1 v2

def hus (G : CDMG Node) (v1 v2 : Node) : Prop :=
  G.hut v1 v2 ∨ G.huh v1 v2

def sus (G : CDMG Node) (v1 v2 : Node) : Prop :=
  G.tuh v1 v2 ∨ G.hut v1 v2 ∨ G.huh v1 v2
lean_explanation:
This file formalises the seven shorthand notations of LN block `not-cdmg`: the ambient-vertex predicate `v ∈ G` and six edge relations `\tuh`, `\hut`, `\huh`, `\suh`, `\hus`, `\sus`. The encodings live under the `Causality.CDMG` namespace, sharing the binder context `{Node : Type*} [DecidableEq Node]` (the ambient vertex type and its decidable equality, both inherited from the `CDMG` structure in `def_3_1`). Each declaration takes the graph `G : CDMG Node` as an explicit first argument so that the dot-notation `G.tuh v1 v2` mirrors the LN's `$v_1 \tuh v_2 \in G$`.

Item 1 is a `Membership Node (CDMG Node)` typeclass instance. Lean's `Membership` class is the dispatch hook for the `∈` symbol: registering this instance is what lets the literal text `v ∈ G` parse and elaborate. The body `v ∈ G.J ∪ G.V` reuses Mathlib's `Finset.instMembership` on the right-hand `∈`, so the two `∈`s in `(v ∈ G) ↔ (v ∈ G.J ∪ G.V)` are dispatched to different instances by Lean's typeclass resolution — no ascription needed.

Items 2–4 are primitive `Prop`-valued definitions: `G.tuh v1 v2 := (v1, v2) ∈ G.E` (directed forward), `G.hut v1 v2 := (v2, v1) ∈ G.E` (directed backward, same `E`, swapped pair), and `G.huh v1 v2 := (v1, v2) ∈ G.L` (bidirected). Each unfolds to a literal `Finset` membership on the corresponding `CDMG` edge field.

Items 5–7 are the LN's "star" disjunctions: `G.suh v1 v2 := G.tuh v1 v2 ∨ G.huh v1 v2` (head at `v2`, anything at `v1`), `G.hus v1 v2 := G.hut v1 v2 ∨ G.huh v1 v2` (head at `v1`, anything at `v2`), and `G.sus v1 v2 := G.tuh v1 v2 ∨ G.hut v1 v2 ∨ G.huh v1 v2` (anything-anything, i.e. adjacency). These are plain logical disjunctions, ordered to match the LN's enumeration so `rcases` patterns read in step with the prose. Symmetry properties (`huh`'s argument-symmetry, `sus`'s full symmetry, the matched-pair `suh ↔ hus` identity) are not baked into the definitions; they fall out of `CDMG`'s `hL_symm` field and a one-line lemma each.
design_choices:
**Membership instance for item 1, not a plain `def`.** The LN writes `v \in G` verbatim at every downstream use. A plain `def CDMG.Mem` would force every consumer to write `G.Mem v`. Registering a `Membership Node (CDMG Node)` instance dispatches Lean's `∈` symbol to our `mem` function directly, so the LN syntax parses on the nose. There is no clash with Mathlib's `Finset` membership: the second type argument differs, so typeclass resolution picks the right instance at each call site.

**Six separate predicates, not a single inductive `EdgeType`.** A unified `inductive EdgeType := tailHead | headTail | headHead` with a derived `G.edgeOf` would collapse the three primitive edge shapes into one constructor sum. Downstream consumers, however, write set-builder forms `{w | w \tuh v \in G}` (parent sets), walk-edge predicates, and acyclicity chains `v_0 \tuh v_1 \tuh \dots \tuh v_n` that pattern-match against individual edge symbols. Centralising into `EdgeType` would force a `match` split at every use site and would obscure the symbol-per-relation grep correspondence between LN macros and Lean identifiers.

**Plain `def`, not `abbrev` and no `notation` macro.** An `abbrev` would auto-unfold to the body at every elaboration site, eliminating the named abstraction the LN introduces — particularly visible for items 5–7, where each use site would balloon into a two- or three-way disjunction. A `notation v1 →ᵍ v2` macro was considered and rejected: every infix would have to carry an explicit `[G]` annotation (downstream rows compare a graph to a rewritten copy), and the resulting `v1 →[G] v2` is visually heavier than `G.tuh v1 v2`. Dot-notation also keeps the grep match to the LN macro names exact.

**`hut` as a primitive `def`, not `G.tuh v2 v1` or `tuh ∘ swap`.** The LN treats `\hut` as a primitive macro, and `def_3_3` distinguishes 'edges of the form `v_1 \hut v_2`' as a syntactic shape. A composition spelling would force every consumer to peel off `Function.comp_apply` and `Prod.swap_apply` before the underlying `Finset` lemma can fire. The definitional equality `G.hut v1 v2 = G.tuh v2 v1` is then a trivial one-line lemma.

**Symmetry kept outside the definitions.** `huh` stores the literal `(v1, v2) ∈ G.L`, even though the LN intends `huh` to be symmetric in its two arguments. The symmetry is a consequence of `CDMG.hL_symm` (the `CDMG` structure's symmetry constraint on `L`), exposed as a one-line lemma rather than inlined into `huh`. Inlining would make `huh` and the underlying `L`-membership drift apart, forcing every `hL_symm` invocation downstream to also rewrite through `huh`.

**Three disjuncts for `sus`, not four — `tut` is excluded by CDMG definition.** Mechanically reading 'the star stands for arrowhead or tail' would suggest a fourth, tail-tail disjunct. CDMGs admit only directed (`E`) and bidirected (`L`) edges, so no tail-tail edge type exists; the LN therefore enumerates three disjuncts. Any downstream `rcases` on `G.sus` may assume exhaustiveness over exactly these three cases.
